title: Generation of Internal Antino Acid Sequences without Peptide Purification. Amino Acid Sequencing of the Ethylene Biosynthesis Inducing Xylanase from Trichodernza viride
author_string: F.D. Dean, Jeffrey, M. Coull, James, Anderson, James D.
abstract: A procedure  for the rapid determination  of internal amino acid sequences  from a purified  protein  relies on covalent attachment of the protein to derivatized PVDF membranes followed by cleavage with specific proteases or chemicals. By combining different cleavage protocols with various attachment chemistries, new NH2-termini can easily be generated in various portions of the protein.  Sequencing of insoluble or protease-resistant proteins is facilitated  by the resistance of PVDF membranes to organic solvents.
